title: When Making Decisions: - Pause and Expand: Actively Look for Examples Beyond the Most
subtitle: Overcome Availability Bias
description:
When making decisions:
- Pause and expand: Actively look for examples beyond the most recent or visible ones.
- Balance sources: Include diverse or older sources of information in your analysis.
- Ask questions: “Am I focusing on what’s easiest to recall, or what’s truly relevant?”

Example: Before deciding on a restaurant, research a few options instead of just recalling the last one you visited.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key metrics to track, such as the number of decisions changed and time spent on the pause-and-expand habit. This will help measure the effectiveness of the practice.

howTo.title: Choose a decision to practice
howTo.summary: Select a decision you will make today, such as dinner or a small purchase. This will be your opportunity to apply the pause-and-expand technique.

howTo.title: Pause and expand
howTo.summary: Take 60–120 seconds to pause and retrieve three alternative examples from different times or sources. Evaluate these options before making your final decision.

howTo.title: Log your decision
howTo.summary: Record the decision you made, the alternatives considered, and any insights gained from the pause-and-expand process. This will help reinforce the habit.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reflect on the outcomes of your decisions. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/availability-bias-decision-checklist/.
